Container.Name: obsidian-git-backup
Container.Overview:
Syncs an Obsidian vault via obsidian-headless (official Obsidian Sync client) and automatically backs it up to a git repository.
Every time Obsidian Sync delivers changes, they are committed and pushed to your git remote — giving you a private, versioned backup of your vault with full history.
Features: - Continuous sync via obsidian-headless - Filesystem-event-driven git commits (no polling) - Configurable debounce period for large vaults - Automatic SSH key generation on first run - Supports E2E encrypted vaults
Requires an Obsidian Sync subscription and a git repository (GitHub, GitLab, etc).
Run one container per vault. See the project README for full setup instructions.
Container.Requires: An Obsidian Sync subscription and a git repository with SSH access. You must obtain an auth token by running 'ob login' with obsidian-headless installed on any machine.

Config.Name: Obsidian Auth Token
Config.Description: Your Obsidian account auth token. Install obsidian-headless (npm install -g obsidian-headless), run 'ob login', then copy the contents of the auth_token file. WARNING: Anyone with this token can access all your synced vaults.

Config.Name: Vault Name
Config.Description: Name or ID of the remote vault to sync. Run 'ob sync-list-remote' to see available vaults.

Config.Name: Git Remote URL
Config.Description: SSH URL of the git remote to push to. Example: git@github.com:username/vault-backup.git

Config.Name: Config Storage
Config.Description: Persistent config directory. Stores SSH keys and obsidian-headless state. Must survive container restarts or you'll lose your SSH key.

Config.Name: Vault Storage
Config.Description: Vault data and git working tree. Can be re-synced/re-cloned if lost, but persisting avoids re-downloading.

Config.Name: Git Branch
Config.Description: Git branch to commit and push to.

Config.Name: Debounce Seconds
Config.Description: Seconds of filesystem quiet before committing. After detecting changes, the watcher waits this long for more changes before committing. For large vaults, increase to 60+.

Config.Name: E2EE Password
Config.Description: End-to-end encryption password for the vault. Only needed if the vault uses E2E encryption.

Config.Name: PUID
Config.Description: User ID for file permissions. Unraid default is 99 (nobody).

Config.Name: PGID
Config.Description: Group ID for file permissions. Unraid default is 100 (users).
